title: orthopogon sylvaticus
intro: Orthopogon sylvaticus, also known as East Indian lemongrass, is a tropical grass native to the Indian subcontinent and Southeast Asia, characterized by its long, thin leaves and citrusy fragrance. The essential oil extracted from its leaves has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce pain and fever, and promote wound healing. The health benefits of Orthopogon sylvaticus include its potential to alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia, as well as its ability to support immune function and reduce inflammation. Orthopogon sylvaticus can be prepared as a tea, oil, or powder for use in herbal remedies, and is often used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
